reply_sections.text: The provided materials explain how to compute the partial derivative of the cost function with respect to the weights, which is a component of the weight update. However, the materials do not explicitly state or explain why the weight update itself is negative.

reply_sections.heading: Grounded answer
reply_sections.text: Backpropagation moves in a backward direction because the process starts with the final loss value and works toward the input to determine how each weight affects that loss. According to the chain rule, calculating the gradient for a weight early in the network requires

quality_summary.notes: Short student notes with a few paraphrases

bullets: Cache activations in the forward pass
Track the sign on the learning update
Compare each step with the slide deck
slides.snippet_text: Remember to save intermediate activations, keep the minus sign in the update, and check each derivative term.
slides.title: Student recap

quality_summary.notes: Lecture deck imported cleanly in fixture mode

bullets: Explain why gradients are propagated from the loss backward
Connect local derivatives to global updates
Identify the role of the learning rate
slides.snippet_text: Learning objectives: explain the chain rule, compute a backward pass, and apply gradient descent updates.
slides.title: Backpropagation Essentials

bullets: Write the output as a composition of intermediate functions
Differentiate one edge at a time
Multiply local gradients to get the total derivative
slides.snippet_text: The chain rule breaks a complicated derivative into local derivatives that can be multiplied along the computation path.
slides.title: Chain Rule Intuition

bullets: Forward pass computes activations and loss
Each layer passes gradient information to the previous layer
slides.snippet_text: Store intermediate activations during the forward pass so the backward pass can reuse them when computing gradients.
slides.title: Forward Pass and Backward Pass

bullets: Use the learning rate eta to control step size
Subtract the gradient to reduce the loss
Check tensor shapes before applying the update
slides.snippet_text: Parameter update: w <- w - eta * dL/dw. The minus sign matters because we are descending the loss surface.
slides.title: Gradient Descent Update

bullets: Do not skip intermediate terms in the chain rule
Do not add the gradient when the slide uses subtraction
Verify activation and weight matrix dimensions
slides.snippet_text: Common errors include dropping an intermediate derivative, reversing the sign of the update, and ignoring dimension checks.
slides.title: Common Mistakes
